name: Domain
author: Parker Brothers (1983); rules & analysis by Larry Back (Abstract Games #12) / AGP
description: A tile version of Othello (Larry Back, Abstract Games #12; published in North America by Parker Brothers, 1983 — earlier Boomerang / Kiss / Chameleon in Europe). On a 9x9 board each player holds the same 26 reversible polyomino tiles (short/medium/long bars, angles, squares, small and large T's and crosses, covering 2 to 5 squares each). Place one of your tiles over empty squares; every opponent tile orthogonally touching it flips to your colour (diagonal touches never count). Except for the opening, a tile must touch at least one enemy tile. When neither player can move, whoever covers the most squares wins; an equal cover is an honest draw. Three rule versions: a common-pool basic game, the default intermediate game, and an advanced version where every touched tile — yours and your opponent's — flips.
